snippet: Gillette Stadium Bag Policy for FIFA World Cup 2026  surprisesports
content:
Gillette Stadium in Foxborough, Massachusetts, enforces a clear bag policy for all events, including FIFA World Cup 2026 matches.
Whether you’re attending a Patriots game, concert, or international soccer tournament, understanding the bag requirements prevents entry delays and rejections at security checkpoints.
This guide covers exactly what bags pass inspection, prohibited items, and how to prepare without hassle.
Quick Reference: Gillette Stadium Bag Policy Compliance Chart
|Bag Type||Size Limit||Material||Status||Notes|
|Clear backpack||12″ × 6″ × 12″||Plastic, vinyl, PVC||✅ Allowed||Transparent frame required|
|Clear fanny pack||12″ × 6″ × 12″||Plastic, vinyl, PVC||✅ Allowed||One-gallon freezer bag alternative|
|Small clutch||4.5″ × 6.5″||Any (opaque OK)||✅ Allowed||Wallet-sized only|
|Diaper bag (clear)||12″ × 6″ × 12″||Plastic, vinyl, PVC||✅ Allowed||Subject to inspection|
|Standard backpack||Any||Any||❌ Prohibited||Even if under size limit|
|Opaque handbag||Over 4.5″ × 6.5″||Any||❌ Prohibited||Must go to storage|
|Rolling luggage||Any||Any||❌ Prohibited||Use external storage|
|Camera bag (non-clear)||Any||Any||❌ Prohibited||Only if transparent and compliant|
What Bags Are Allowed: The Clear Bag Policy at Gillette Stadium
Gillette Stadium enforces a clear bag policy consistent with NFL guidelines and FIFA security standards.
The policy is straightforward but specific. Understanding the exact dimensions and materials accepted prevents rejection at the gates. This policy applies to all events, from Patriots games to concerts and World Cup matches.
Clear Bag Requirements & Size Limits (12″ × 6″ × 12″)
Clear bags are the primary option for bringing items into Gillette Stadium. The maximum permitted size is 12 inches by 6 inches by 12 inches. In metric, that’s 30.5 × 15.25 × 30.5 centimeters.
This size accommodates clear backpacks, fanny packs, and cinch bags as long as they fit within these exact dimensions. Many bags marketed as “stadium-compliant” actually exceed this limit by a few inches. Measure before you go.
A one-gallon clear plastic freezer bag also qualifies if it stays completely transparent and doesn’t exceed the size threshold. These are cheap, accessible alternatives if you don’t own a compliant bag.
Small Clutches & Wristlets (6.5″ × 4.5″ Max)
Non-clear bags are permitted only if they stay under 6.5 inches by 4.5 inches. This is roughly palm-of-your-hand sizing. Think wallet-sized clutches, small crossbody bags, or minimalist pouches.
Handles and straps are acceptable. The overall dimensions are what matter. A crossbody clutch worn under your shirt works, though security may ask you to display it for verification.
One-Gallon Freezer Bags Permitted
A standard gallon-sized clear freezer bag meets Gillette’s policy requirements. These cost under $2 and offer a temporary solution if you don’t have a compliant bag. They’re genuinely see-through and fit the size specs.
Fill it with only essentials: ID, ticket, phone, wallet. Nothing more. Security inspects these just like any other bag.
- Read Also: AT&T Stadium Bag Policy
What You Cannot Bring: Prohibited Items List at Gillette
Gillette Stadium maintains a comprehensive prohibited items list aligned with NFL and FIFA security standards.
According to NFL event safety guidelines, these restrictions exist to manage crowds, prevent security threats, and ensure evacuation safety. Gillette enforces this policy strictly across all 75,000 seats.
Backpacks, Suitcases & Travel Luggage Banned
Standard backpacks are prohibited regardless of size. This includes school backpacks, hiking packs, travel daypacks, and messenger bags with opaque frames.
The only backpack exception is a clear backpack meeting the 12×6×12 inch size limit with a fully transparent frame. Most backpacks have opaque padding or frames that automatically disqualify them.
Suitcases, rolling luggage, hard-sided cases, and overnight bags are all banned. These items block sightlines and create evacuation delays. If you’re traveling from out of town, check luggage at your hotel before arriving at the stadium.
Cameras & Recording Devices (Video Prohibited)
Personal phones are allowed. Professional cameras with detachable lenses longer than 6 inches are prohibited. This includes DSLRs, mirrorless cameras, and telephoto setups.
Tripods, monopods, and selfie sticks cannot enter. Drones and remote-controlled devices are banned. Professional audio recorders and Go-Pro cameras with mounting equipment are prohibited.
Weapons, Coolers & Food Restrictions
Weapons of any kind are banned. This includes firearms, ammunition, pellet guns, knives, box cutters, pepper spray, tear gas, mace, tasers, and stun guns.
Coolers, thermoses, and beverage containers are prohibited. You can purchase drinks inside the stadium. Flammable liquids, explosives, fireworks, and any suspicious devices are completely banned.
Special Policy Questions: Specific Items
Users often ask about items not explicitly mentioned in the main policy. Here are the specific answers.
Can You Bring a Water Bottle to Gillette Stadium?
Empty water bottles are generally allowed. You can bring an empty, reusable water bottle and fill it at water fountains inside the stadium. Some venues have water bottle refill stations.
Sealed bottles from outside may be confiscated. Check with Guest Services closer to your event for current policies on beverages.
Are Crossbody Bags Allowed at Gillette?
Crossbody bags are allowed if they meet the size requirements. If your crossbody bag is under 4.5×6.5 inches, it can be non-clear. If it’s larger, it must be clear and fit the 12×6×12 inch limit.
Test your bag dimensions before game day. Measure the main compartment, not including straps or handles. Many crossbody bags exceed limits without the owner realizing it.
Diaper Bag Exceptions for Parents
Parents can bring clear diaper bags that comply with the 12×6×12 inch policy. The bag must be transparent for inspection purposes.
Formula, baby food, medications, and inhalers are permitted after security inspects them. Medical equipment bags with oxygen, glucose monitors, or EpiPens receive similar treatment. Arrive early with documentation so staff can verify contents without delays.
For questions, contact Gillette Stadium Guest Services at (508) 543-1776.
Variations by Event Type: Event-Specific Rules at Gillette
Gillette Stadium hosts multiple event types, and some have slightly different rules depending on the event. Clear bag policy requirements remain consistent, but timing and crowd expectations vary by event type.
Gillette Patriots Game Bag Policy
NFL game days follow the standard clear bag policy at Gillette. The 12×6×12 inch clear bag limit applies. Small clutches under 4.5×6.5 inches are permitted.
Alcohol cannot be brought into Gillette Stadium. Beer and other beverages must be purchased inside. Arrive 90 minutes before kickoff to account for security screening during Patriots home games.
Bag Rules for Concerts & Events
Concert and entertainment events sometimes have modified bag policies at Gillette. Some artists or promoters may enforce stricter restrictions. Check the specific event’s website before arrival.
In general, assume the standard Gillette policy applies unless announced otherwise.
FIFA World Cup 2026 Enhanced Security
FIFA World Cup 2026 matches at Gillette will implement enhanced international security protocols. Expect stricter enforcement and longer screening times compared to regular season events.
The clear bag policy will remain the same, but staff will be more thorough with inspections. Arrive at least two to three hours before kickoff for World Cup matches. International visitor volumes will be significantly higher than typical events.
How Strictly Is the Policy Enforced?
The policy is enforced consistently. Knowing what happens if your bag doesn’t comply helps you plan accordingly.
Stadium Security Inspections & Procedures
Security personnel visually inspect clear bags without opening them when possible. Metal detectors scan for prohibited items. Pat-downs confirm compliance with policy.
Most fans with compliant bags clear security in under 30 seconds. Non-compliant bags trigger extended inspections, manual opening, and potential denial of entry. The process is faster if you arrive early and have a bag that clearly meets the size and transparency requirements.
Mobile Locker Options for Non-Compliant Bags
If your bag doesn’t meet the policy, external storage options exist. The Mobile Locker Company operates secure lockers outside the stadium.
Pricing: $10-$15 per bag (varies by event). You check in your non-compliant bag, receive a digital claim ID on your phone, and collect it after the event. Check-in takes about 90 seconds. Email them at hello@themobilelockerco.com for pricing on your specific event date.
Locker availability runs from 2 hours before event start until 1.5 hours after the event concludes. Items left overnight are held securely. Contact The Mobile Locker Company if you need to retrieve items from previous events. Gates 3 and 4 have convenient locker access.
What Happens if You Violate the Policy?
Security will deny entry with a non-compliant bag. You have two options: use the Mobile Locker Company or go without the bag.
Attempting to sneak prohibited items into the stadium results in ejection and possible arrest. The policy is non-negotiable for safety and security reasons. Don’t test it.
- Read Also: NRG Stadium Bag Policy
Additional Stadium Information
Getting to Gillette Stadium and understanding the broader logistics matters for your visit.
Parking at Gillette Stadium
Gillette offers extensive parking with over 20,000 spaces. Parking costs vary by event, typically $15-$30. Arrive early for premium parking locations closer to the stadium.
If you’re taking public transportation, MBTA commuter rail serves the area. The Providence/Stoughton line has a station near Gillette. Rideshare services are also available from nearby towns.
Mobile Ticketing Requirements
Mobile tickets are standard at Gillette. Download the official NFL or event app and have your ticket ready on your phone. Physical tickets are rarely accepted.
Arrive with your phone fully charged. Have your ticket visible at entry gates. Mobile ticketing speeds up the entry process compared to searching for physical tickets.
Getting to Foxborough from Boston
Foxborough is about 35 miles southwest of Boston. Driving takes 45 minutes to an hour depending on traffic. Take I-95 South to Route 1 South, then follow stadium signs.
From Boston’s South Station, the MBTA commuter rail reaches Gillette’s area. The trip takes about 60 minutes. Plan extra time on game days when public transit is crowded. Rideshare from Boston costs $30-$50 depending on demand.
FAQs
Is there a clear bag policy at Gillette Stadium for FIFA World Cup matches?
Yes. All bags must either be clear plastic, vinyl, or PVC measuring no more than 12×6×12 inches, or non-clear bags under 4.5×6.5 inches. Gillette Stadium’s official policy requires transparent visibility of contents for both regular season and World Cup 2026 matches.
Can I bring a standard backpack into Gillette Stadium?
No. Standard backpacks are prohibited regardless of size. Clear backpacks meeting the 12×6×12 inch limit with fully transparent frames are the only exception. Opaque backpacks will be denied at entry.
What happens if my bag doesn’t comply with Gillette’s policy?
Security will turn you away. The Mobile Locker Company operates storage outside the stadium for $10-$15 per bag. You can retrieve it after the event. Contact them at hello@themobilelockerco.com for pricing details.
Are diaper bags allowed at Gillette Stadium?
Yes. Diaper bags must be clear and meet the 12×6×12 inch size requirement. Formula, baby food, medications, and medical supplies are permitted after inspection.
What’s the maximum size for a non-clear bag or clutch at Gillette?
Non-clear bags are allowed only if under 4.5×6.5 inches, roughly palm-sized. This includes wallet clutches and small crossbody bags. Anything larger must be clear and meet the 12×6×12 inch limit.
Can I bring a camera or personal phone to Gillette Stadium?
Personal phones are allowed. Professional cameras with detachable lenses longer than 6 inches are prohibited. Tripods, recording devices, and mounting equipment are banned. Check the official guidelines for questions about specific equipment or call Guest Services at (508) 543-1776.

title: Hard Rock Stadium Bag Policy for FIFA World Cup 2026 - surprisesports
snippet: Hard Rock Stadium Bag Policy for FIFA World Cup 2026  surprisesports
content:
Confusion about stadium bag rules causes stress at security. Arriving with a prohibited bag means denied entry or forced locker fees. This guide explains which bags work, which are banned, and what exceptions apply.
At Hard Rock Stadium, clear bags must not exceed 12 inches by 6 inches by 12 inches. Small bags under 4.5 inches by 6.5 inches can be any material.
According to Hard Rock Stadium’s official policy, transparent material is required for larger bags only. Backpacks are completely prohibited regardless of size.
Quick Reference: Hard Rock Stadium Bag Policy Compliance Chart
|Bag Type||Size Limit||Material||Status|
|Clear plastic bag||12″ x 6″ x 12″||Plastic, vinyl, PVC||✓ Allowed|
|Small clutch||4.5″ x 6.5″||Any||✓ Allowed|
|Small crossbody||4.5″ x 6.5″||Any||✓ Allowed|
|Clear fanny pack||12″ x 6″ x 12″||Plastic, vinyl, PVC||✓ Allowed|
|Diaper bag (clear)||12″ x 6″ x 12″||Plastic, vinyl, PVC||✓ Allowed|
|Diaper bag (opaque)||Standard size||Any||✓ Allowed*|
|Backpack||Any size||Any||✗ Prohibited|
|Large purse||Over 4.5″ x 6.5″||Any||✗ Prohibited|
|Luggage/suitcase||Any||Any||✗ Prohibited|
*Diaper bags subject to inspection.
Clear Bag Policy: What You Need to Know
Bag Dimensions and Transparency Requirements
All bags exceeding 4.5 inches by 6.5 inches must be completely transparent. Security scans clear bags instantly without opening them, which speeds entry and reduces wait times. Tinted, frosted, or colored bags don’t qualify.
Smaller bags under 4.5 inches by 6.5 inches can be leather, fabric, metal, or any non-transparent material. Clutches, small crossbody bags, and compact purses that fit this dimension are welcome in any color.
How to Measure Your Bag
Measure length, width, and depth in inches before arriving. A standard envelope measures 8.5 by 11 inches. Your bag should be smaller in at least two dimensions. Borderline bags may be denied by security staff.
Security Scanning Process
All bags go through security inspection at entry gates. Clear bags are scanned visually without opening (15-30 seconds). Non-clear small bags are opened and inspected quickly. Security has discretion on borderline sizes.
Prohibited Items: What’s Banned
Backpacks Are Completely Banned
Backpacks of all sizes are completely prohibited regardless of dimensions. School backpacks, daypacks, hiking packs, and all backpack-style bags are denied entry. This is a category exclusion, not a size issue.
Large Purses Must Be Clear
Purses exceeding 4.5 inches by 6.5 inches must be transparent plastic, vinyl, or PVC. Standard shoulder bags, totes, and oversized handbags fall under this restriction. Large opaque purses are not permitted.
Luggage and Travel Gear
Luggage, suitcases, duffel bags, gym bags, and travel gear are prohibited. Even small rolling luggage isn’t allowed. If your bag suggests travel, it won’t be permitted.
- Read Also: MetLife Stadium Bag Policy
Alternatives to Backpacks
Fanny Packs as Backpack Replacement
Fanny packs not exceeding 4.5 inches by 6.5 inches are allowed in any material. Standard belt fanny packs, hip packs, and compact crossbody fanny packs that fit this dimension carry phones, wallets, keys, sunscreen, and essentials.
Modern fanny packs come in various styles. Leather, fabric, nylon, and structured designs are all acceptable. The size limit matters more than the style.
Small Crossbody Bags
Crossbody bags are allowed only if they measure 4.5 inches by 6.5 inches or smaller. Many modern crossbody bags are designed as compact bags, making them compliant. Larger crossbody bags marketed for travel will not fit policy.
Measure before purchasing. Ask retailers for exact length, width, and depth dimensions. This prevents arriving with a non-compliant bag.
Clutches and Small Purses
Clutch bags and small purses not exceeding 4.5 inches by 6.5 inches are permitted in any material. Structured metal-frame clutches, soft fabric clutches, and minimalist wallets fall within acceptable dimensions.
The material can be leather, fabric, vinyl, metal, or any combination. Color and design have no restrictions. A classic black leather clutch or colorful patterned purse works if it fits the size requirement.
Diaper Bag Exception: What Parents Need to Know
Exemption From Clear Bag Requirement
Diaper bags are exempt from the clear bag requirement but still subject to size limits. Standard diaper bag sizes (approximately 16 inches by 12 inches by 8 inches) are acceptable. Security will ask what you’re carrying and may request a visual inspection.
The exception exists so parents don’t face delays. Standard backpack-style diaper bags are generally accepted without issue. Pack only actual diaper supplies inside.
Inspection Process for Diaper Bags
Security will conduct a visual and potentially manual inspection to verify the contents are diaper items. This process takes 30-45 seconds, slightly longer than clear bag inspection. Cooperation and honesty speed the process.
If you’re carrying medical supplies, mention this upfront to expedite approval. Staff understand that medical needs and infant supplies are legitimate.
Medical Bags: Documentation and Approval
Medical bags for prescribed medications, mobility aids, or oxygen equipment may enter in non-clear bags with staff approval. Prescription labels and medical documentation available prevents delays, though not required.
Contact Hard Rock Stadium guest services ahead of your event if concerned about specific equipment. Pre-approval eliminates checkpoint delays entirely.
- Read Also: Lumen Field Bag Policy
Entry Process and Security Procedures
All bags go through security inspection at designated checkpoints. Clear bags are scanned visually (15-30 seconds). Non-clear small bags are opened for quick inspection. The process is mandatory for all attendees.
Security personnel have discretion when bags measure close to size limits. Borderline bags might be denied by one officer but accepted by another. Staff are trained on policy but apply judgment.
Peak Entry Times and Wait Management
Peak entry times (30 minutes before event start) see longest waits because multiple security lanes operate simultaneously with high volume.
Arriving 45-60 minutes early cuts security wait time significantly. Off-peak entry (2-3 hours before start) is nearly instant.
The fastest entry is arriving bag-free. Bagless attendees skip security entirely and enter within seconds.
What Happens If Your Bag Doesn’t Comply
Security will deny entry with non-compliant bags. Your options are returning to your vehicle, checking it at stadium lockers ($15-20), or giving it to a friend outside the venue. No exceptions exist once at security.
Bag Storage Solutions
Hard Rock Stadium operates lockers at designated locations on the East and Southwest sides. Rental costs $15-20 depending on locker size. Lockers fit most handbags, small backpacks, and jackets.
Availability is not guaranteed at high-capacity events. Arriving one hour before event start increases chances of finding available lockers. Operating hours typically extend 2-3 hours before events through 30 minutes after conclusion.
Benefits of Checking Your Bag at Lockers
Checked bags let you skip security entirely and enter faster. This trade-off makes sense during peak entry times when security waits are 10-15 minutes. Smaller crowds mean minimal difference.
Third-Party Storage Services
Third-party services like Stasher and LuggageHero operate near the venue. Fees typically range $8-15 per day. Off-site storage requires retrieving your bag after the event, adding time to departure.
For events where you plan nearby activities after, off-site storage makes sense. For straightforward stadium visits, stadium lockers are more convenient.
FAQs
Can I bring a purse to Hard Rock Stadium?
Yes, if it measures 4.5 inches by 6.5 inches or smaller. Small clutches and compact purses are allowed in any material. Larger purses must be clear plastic, vinyl, or PVC and cannot exceed 12 inches by 6 inches by 12 inches. Measure before arriving.
Are crossbody bags allowed at Hard Rock Stadium?
Crossbody bags are allowed only if they measure 4.5 inches by 6.5 inches or smaller. The strap style doesn’t matter. Only dimensions determine compliance. Larger crossbody bags must meet the clear bag requirement.
Can I bring a backpack to Hard Rock Stadium?
No. Backpacks of all sizes are completely prohibited regardless of dimensions. Use a fanny pack or small bag instead. This applies to all backpack-style bags without exception.
Will the FIFA World Cup 2026 use different rules?
FIFA World Cup matches at Hard Rock Stadium will use the same clear bag policy: 12 inches by 6 inches by 12 inches for clear bags, 4.5 inches by 6.5 inches for non-clear small bags. FIFA may add extra security, so plan conservatively.
What if I bring a prohibited bag?
Security will deny entry. You can return to your vehicle, check it at lockers, or give it to someone outside. No refunds or exceptions exist. Plan ahead with a compliant bag.
Can I bring a medical bag?
Yes. Medical bags for medications, mobility aids, or oxygen equipment are exempt. Mention your medical need at security for faster approval.

title: Mercedes-Benz Stadium Bag Policy for FIFA World Cup 2026 - surprisesports
snippet: Mercedes-Benz Stadium Bag Policy for FIFA World Cup 2026  surprisesports
content:
Atlanta is hosting FIFA World Cup 2026, and Mercedes-Benz Stadium will draw fans from around the globe.
Before you arrive at the gates, understand the clear bag policy that governs entry. This isn’t just a stadium preference. It’s a safety standard that FIFA enforces across all tournament venues to manage security efficiently and protect the international crowd.
Knowing what bags pass inspection means avoiding entry delays, missed moments, and the frustration of turning back after traveling to the stadium. Whether you’re a first-time visitor or seasoned supporter, this guide answers every question about what you can bring inside.
Quick Reference: Mercedes-Benz Stadium Bag Policy Compliance Chart
|Bag Type||Size Limit||Material||Status||Notes|
|Clear backpack||12″ × 6″ × 12″||Plastic, vinyl, PVC||✅ Allowed||Transparent frame required|
|Clear fanny pack||12″ × 6″ × 12″||Plastic, vinyl, PVC||✅ Allowed||One-gallon freezer bag alternative|
|Small clutch||4.5″ × 6.5″||Any (opaque OK)||✅ Allowed||Wallet-sized only|
|Diaper bag (clear)||12″ × 6″ × 12″||Plastic, vinyl, PVC||✅ Allowed||Subject to inspection|
|Standard backpack||Any||Any||❌ Prohibited||Even if under size limit|
|Opaque handbag||Over 4.5″ × 6.5″||Any||❌ Prohibited||Must go to storage|
|Rolling luggage||Any||Any||❌ Prohibited||Use Mobile Locker Company|
|Camera bag (non-clear)||Any||Any||❌ Prohibited||Only if transparent and compliant|
Why Clear Bags Are Required for FIFA Security
Clear bags aren’t an inconvenience.
They’re a deliberate safety mechanism that FIFA mandates at all World Cup venues. Unlike typical NFL events at the same stadium, FIFA tournaments require elevated security due to international attendance and the global visibility of the event.
Transparent visibility allows security personnel to inspect bag contents without opening them, which accelerates screening and reduces bottlenecks at entry gates during peak arrival times.
FIFA vs. NFL Security Standards
The difference matters because FIFA operates under distinct international security protocols.
While the same stadium hosts both NFL and soccer events, FIFA implements stricter standards aligned with global safety requirements. Transparent bags let security confirm what’s inside in seconds rather than minutes.
This distinction isn’t just procedural. It reflects how FIFA coordinates security across three countries (USA, Canada, Mexico) and ensures consistent screening regardless of a visitor’s home country or language.
How Clear Bags Accelerate Entry
Security personnel can visually confirm bag contents without hand searches. Metal detectors scan for prohibited items, and pat-downs confirm compliance.
Most fans clear screening in 30 seconds with a compliant bag. Non-compliant bags trigger extended inspections, manual opening, and potential denial.
The sports planning guide for World Cup 2026 emphasizes arriving 90 minutes early to account for screening lines during high-volume events like quarter-final matches.
Airport-Style Screening Explained
Think of stadium security like airport TSA checkpoints. You place your bag on a conveyor belt leading through a metal detector. Staff visually inspect the clear interior. If anything looks questionable, you’ll be asked to explain or remove the item.
This system works only if bags are actually transparent. Bring hand-sized essentials: ID, ticket, phone, wallet. The faster you move through, the more time you have to enjoy the fan experience before kickoff.
- Read Also: Gillette Stadium Bag Policy
Allowed Bag Sizes and Materials
The clear bag policy has two tiers: primary and secondary. Primary bags (clear, large) must meet strict dimensions. Secondary bags (small, non-clear) are allowed only if they stay within clutch size limits.
Clear Bags: 12×6×12 Inches
This is the maximum permitted size for clear plastic, vinyl, or PVC bags.
In centimeters, that’s 30.5 × 15.25 × 30.5 cm.
The specification includes clear backpacks, fanny packs, and cinch bags as long as they fit within these dimensions. Many bags marketed as “stadium-compliant” exceed this limit by a few inches.
Measure yours before game day.
A gallon-sized freezer bag also qualifies if completely clear and under the size threshold.
Small Clutches: 4.5×6.5 Inches
Non-clear bags are allowed only if they don’t exceed this tiny footprint.
This means wallet-sized clutches, small crossbody bags, or minimalist pouches. Think of it as palm-of-your-hand sizing.
Handles and straps are fine as long as the overall dimensions stay under 4.5×6.5 inches. A crossbody clutch worn under your shirt works, though security may ask you to show it’s compliant.
Approved Materials (Plastic, Vinyl, PVC)
Only transparent, flexible materials pass inspection.
Tinted, frosted, or patterned bags are not allowed even if they’re technically see-through. Simple logos on one side are acceptable. Complex graphics that obstruct interior visibility will be denied.
Avoid bags with metallic hardware, rigid frames, or decorative elements that block the view inside. If you can’t see all interior contents from every angle, it fails.
Approved bag materials:
- Clear plastic (most common)
- Vinyl (flexible, durable)
- PVC (rigid clarity, airport-style)
- One-gallon freezer bags (temporary option)
- Transparent backpacks (if under size limit)
Specific Bag Types and Exceptions
Some bags need special consideration, and certain populations have legitimate exceptions to standard rules.
Diaper Bags and Medical Bags
Parents traveling with infants and toddlers can bring clear diaper bags that comply with size policy.
The bag must be clear and meet the 12×6×12 inch limit.
Security will inspect diaper bags just like any other. Formula, baby food, medications, and inhalers are exempted after proper inspection.
Medical equipment bags (oxygen, glucose monitors, EpiPens) receive similar treatment. Arrive early with documentation if you’re carrying specialized medical gear so staff can verify contents without delays.
Non-Clear Bags Rules
The only way to bring an opaque bag is if it stays under 4.5×6.5 inches.
Larger opaque bags mean entry denied, no exceptions.
Some fans ask if they can bring a non-clear bag in addition to a clear bag. The answer is yes, but the non-clear bag must be small enough to fit inside or carry separately while still complying with size limits. You can’t use a large opaque bag as your primary carrier.
Transparency and Hardware Requirements
Every angle of your bag’s interior must be visible without opening it.
Buckles, grommets, or metal décor that conceals interior space will get your bag rejected at the gate. Simpler designs pass faster.
If your bag has opaque side pockets or hidden compartments, it won’t meet the visual inspection standard. Test your bag at home by looking through it from all sides. If you can’t see the entire interior from the outside, security won’t let it through.
Prohibited Items and Restrictions
Some bags are outright banned, and certain items can’t be brought inside regardless of bag type.
Backpacks Not Allowed
Standard backpacks are prohibited.
This applies to school backpacks, hiking packs, and travel daypacks. The one exception: clear backpacks that don’t exceed 12×6×12 inches.
The frame and straps must also be transparent or at least not obstruct the view inside. Most backpacks, even small ones, have opaque frames or padding that makes them non-compliant.
Large Luggage Restrictions
Suitcases, rolling luggage, hard-sided cases, and overnight bags are all banned.
These items create evacuation delays and block sightlines. If you’re traveling from out of town, check your luggage at your hotel or use external storage services before heading to the stadium.
This rule applies regardless of whether luggage is locked or empty.
Item-Specific Bans
Weapons and safety items:
- Explosives, fireworks, flares
- Firearms, ammunition, pellet guns
- Knives, box cutters, sharp objects
- Pepper spray, tear gas, mace
- Tasers, stun guns, cattle prods
Recording and professional equipment:
- Cameras with lenses longer than 6 inches
- Tripods, monopods, selfie sticks
- Drones and remote-controlled devices
- Professional audio recorders
- Go-Pro cameras and mounting equipment
Prohibited substances:
- Alcohol (beer, wine, spirits)
- Illegal drugs and controlled substances
- Loose batteries outside working devices
- Flammable liquids, lighter fluid
- Any explosive or dangerous materials
Other restricted items:
- Noisemakers (vuvuzelas, air horns, whistles)
- Umbrellas of any kind
- Portable chairs, stools, booster seats
- Selfie sticks and pole-mounted items
- Flags larger than 1.5 meters
- Masks that conceal identity
- Tobacco and vaping products
- Glass bottles, aluminum cans
- Any item security deems suspicious
What to Do If Your Bag Doesn’t Comply
You show up with a non-compliant bag.
Security turns you away. You have options.
External Bag Storage Options
The Mobile Locker Company operates fee-based storage just outside the stadium gates.
Their secure lockers accept bags that don’t meet the clear policy. You check in your bag, receive a digital claim ID on your phone, and collect it after the match.
No pre-booking required. First-come, first-serve.
Lockers are available from 2 hours before kickoff until 1.5 hours after the event ends. Contact The Mobile Locker Company for pricing and specific gate locations.
Mobile Locker Company Details
Locations are positioned near Gates 3 and 4 for convenient access before you hit the main security checkpoint.
Digital check-in takes 90 seconds. Staff answers questions on-site. Items left overnight are held, and retrieval is handled via email.
For most matches, this solution costs $10-$15 per bag depending on event size.
Planning Your Stadium Arrival
The stadium’s recommendation is blunt: don’t bring a bag.
It’s the fastest entry path.
If you must bring essentials, pack only ID, ticket, phone, and wallet in a compliant clear bag or small clutch.
Arrive 90 minutes before kickoff. Plan for 20 to 40 minutes of security screening during high-attendance matches like group-stage finals.
Use public transit via MARTA. Take the Blue/Green Line to GWCC/Vine City station (2-block walk to stadium). Or use rideshare to avoid parking delays and stress.
- Read Also: AT&T Stadium Bag Policy
Before You Head to the Stadium
Plan your bag strategy early.
The simplest approach: skip bags entirely. You’ll clear security fastest with just ID, ticket, and phone.
If you prefer to carry essentials, invest in a clear plastic bag under 12×6×12 inches from any retailer. They cost $5-$15.
Measure your bag.
Don’t estimate. Use a measuring tape on width, depth, and height. One inch over the limit means denial.
Test transparency by looking through it from every angle. If you can’t see the entire interior, it won’t pass inspection.
Arrive 90 minutes early.
Security screening during high-attendance matches takes 20 to 40 minutes. You’re competing with thousands of international fans all entering simultaneously.
Early arrival gives you buffer time, access to bag storage if needed, and time to explore the stadium concourse before kickoff.
Know your prohibited items.
Weapons, explosives, large cameras, umbrellas, noisemakers, alcohol, and flammable materials are banned.
These rules apply to everyone, no exceptions.
If your bag doesn’t comply, use Mobile Locker Company.
They operate outside Gates 3 and 4 with secure digital check-in. Cost is typically $10-$15 per event.
Retrieve your bag up to 1.5 hours after the match concludes.
FAQs
Is there a clear bag policy at Mercedes-Benz Stadium for FIFA matches?
Yes. All bags entering the stadium must either be clear plastic, vinyl, or PVC measuring no more than 12×6×12 inches, or non-clear bags under 4.5×6.5 inches. FIFA security protocols require transparent visibility of bag contents.
Can I bring a backpack into the stadium?
No, standard backpacks are prohibited. Clear backpacks that meet the 12×6×12 inch size limit and have transparent frames are the only exception. Opaque backpacks of any size will be denied entry.
What if I bring a bag that doesn’t comply?
Security will turn you away at the gate. The Mobile Locker Company provides fee-based external storage outside the stadium. You can store non-compliant bags and retrieve them after the match.
Are diaper bags allowed?
Yes, diaper bags must be clear and comply with the 12×6×12 inch size policy. Formula, medications, and baby supplies are permitted after security inspection. Arrive early to allow time for additional screening.
What size is a small, non-clear clutch?
Non-clear bags are allowed only if they don’t exceed 4.5×6.5 inches, about palm-of-your-hand size. This qualifies as a wallet-sized clutch or small crossbody bag. Anything larger must be clear.
Can I bring a camera or phone to record?
Personal phones are allowed. Professional cameras with detachable lenses longer than 6 inches are prohibited. Tripods and recording devices are banned. Check stadium policy if you have questions about specific equipment.

title: The Trionda: Adidas’ mind-blowing smart ball set to revolutionize the 2026 World Cup - Mundo Deportivo
snippet: The Trionda: Adidas’ mind-blowing smart ball set to revolutionize the 2026 World Cup  Mundo Deportivo
content:
The Trionda: Adidas’ mind-blowing smart ball set to revolutionize the 2026 World Cup
TOP
Adidas has introduced the Trionda, an AI-powered match ball for the 2026 FIFA World Cup, symbolizing unity among the U.S., Canada, and Mexico while advancing referee accuracy and performance analytics through cutting-edge technology
- Redacción
Barcelona
Adidas has officially revealed the Trionda, the official match ball for the 2026 FIFA World Cup — a tournament co-hosted by the United States, Canada, and Mexico.
The ball’s name blends “Tri,” representing the trio of host nations, with “onda,” the Spanish word for wave or vibe, reflecting the spirit of energy and connection that defines the event.
Its design incorporates the national colors of all three countries, serving as a visual tribute to the first-ever World Cup jointly hosted across North America.
Cutting-Edge AI Technology Inside the Ball
The Trionda marks a major leap in football innovation with the integration of an AI-powered sensor designed to assist referees and enhance match accuracy.
- The built-in chip delivers real-time data to officials for quicker, more precise decisions.
- It’s the second World Cup ball to feature such technology, following 2022’s Al Rihla.
- Unlike its predecessor, the Trionda’s AI sensor is positioned along the side of the ball rather than the center — a shift aimed at improving signal consistency and tracking precision.
According to CBS Sports, this advancement will provide a wealth of performance data that was previously impossible to capture through optical systems alone.
“The Last Frontier” in Football Analytics
Hannes Schaefke, Adidas’ football innovation lead, described the introduction of AI as a crucial step forward in understanding the game’s evolution.
“If you look at key performance indicators in football — the number of passes, high-speed runs, sprints — all these have increased dramatically,” Schaefke told CBS Sports.
“From a technological perspective, the ball was the last frontier. It’s been nearly impossible to track touch frequency or detailed dribbling data without a sensor inside. Unlocking this information allows for a much deeper understanding of how the game is played — and where it’s heading.”

title: FIFA presents Trionda, smart Adidas ball for 2026 World Cup - Mathrubhumi English
snippet: FIFA presents Trionda, smart Adidas ball for 2026 World Cup  Mathrubhumi English
content:
FIFA has revealed Trionda, the official match ball for the 2026 World Cup, celebrating the three host nations with advanced design and technology
New York: FIFA officially presented Trionda, the ball that will be used in the 2026 World Cup, during a launch event in New York.
The name and design were inspired by the unique hosting arrangement of the tournament across the United States, Mexico and Canada — the first time three countries will jointly stage the competition.
The visual design incorporates key national symbols from each host: Canada’s maple leaves, Mexico’s eagle, and the United States’ stars, all set within a red, blue and green palette. A triangular element signifies the unity of the three nations.
Adidas, the German sportswear giant, has once again manufactured the official match ball, continuing its partnership with FIFA that dates back more than five decades.
Technological features have also been refined. Deep seams ensure improved flight stability, embossed surface icons provide better handling in humid conditions, and a motion sensor chip will capture precise ball data for use in VAR reviews, enhancing the accuracy of refereeing decisions.
Speaking at the launch, FIFA president Gianni Infantino expressed pride in unveiling the ball, saying it embodies the shared identity of the upcoming World Cup.
The 2026 edition will be the first to feature 48 teams, expanding from the traditional 32. FIFA has already begun phased ticket sales online, with more than 4.5 million registrations recorded in the presale draw from fans across 216 nations and territories.
The tournament will run from 11 June to 19 July 2026, with matches spread across venues in the three host countries. The official draw to determine the group stage fixtures will take place on 5 December in Washington.

title: Dallas’ FIFA World Cup 2026 Tech Roundtable Takes the Long View, With a Smart City Focus - Dallas Innovates
snippet: Dallas’ FIFA World Cup 2026 Tech Roundtable Takes the Long View, With a Smart City Focus  Dallas Innovates
content:
Jennifer Sanders, executive director of the North Texas Innovation Alliance, says technology and infrastructure investments made for the FIFA World Cup 2026 can be leveraged to improve the quality of life of North Texas residents long after the event is over.
Earlier this month, the nonprofit group co-hosted a closed technology roundtable with the City of Fort Worth that focused on innovation initiatives around the World Cup. Dallas last year was named a host city for the event—a professional soccer tournament held every four years featuring national soccer teams—for the first time since 1994. And NTXIA, which is a consortium of municipalities, government agencies, tech companies, and academic institutions working to implement a smart region strategy for North Texas, is playing a key role in local preparations for the tournament.
“An event the magnitude of the World Cup presents opportunities to zoom out, get creative, and think big,” Sanders says. “Seizing and shepherding those opportunities is the key.”
Sanders, who also co-founded NTXIA, says the goal of her group is to “thread ‘big idea’ innovation projects throughout World Cup planning” that will have long-range impacts. Those impacts could range from parking and airport logistics to public safety, sustainability, and cybersecurity.
“The implementation of smart city infrastructure and a collaborative tech consortium between cities and municipalities ramping up for the World Cup will have transformative long-term benefits for the region,” Sanders says. “By establishing such innovations now, the region can develop a solid foundation for sustainable urban development and improved quality of life.
“The smart city infrastructure, including sensors, data analytics and connectivity, can be repurposed after the event to address various city challenges like traffic management, energy efficiency, waste management, and public transportation. These are projects that cities have dreamed of implementing that could be realized through the proving ground that this event provides.”
In the realm of public safety, Sanders says innovations and new smart technology could lead to faster response times in case of an emergency at the World Cup.
“For example, using a network of sensors and cameras, authorities can detect crowd density and manage crowd control measures to prevent overcrowding and aid traffic flow, particularly in travel between accommodations, events, and fan-fests occurring across the region,” she says. “Additionally, advanced analytics and predictive models can help anticipate potential security threats, enabling proactive security measures to be deployed effectively.
“Emerging solutions that can identify and facilitate interventions to human trafficking is a critical threat that we also believe technology can support.”
Challenges and opportunities discussed
At the tech roundtable on June 12, NTXIA and the City of Fort Worth hosted representatives from the Dallas Sports Commission and the Fort Worth Sports Commission, as well as representatives from municipalities, regional planning groups, economic development organizations, nonprofits, and the private sector.
While the initial discussion focused on broad challenges and opportunities connected with the 2026 event, future meetings will hone in on the participants’ individual priorities and perspectives.
Sanders says people and organizations interested in inquiring about future World Cup roundtables should contact NTXIA at [email protected].
Dallas is one of the 2026 World Cup’s 16 host cities in three countries, along with Atlanta, Boston, Guadalajara, Houston, Kansas City, Los Angeles, Mexico City, Miami, Monterrey, New York/New Jersey, Philadelphia, San Francisco, Seattle, Toronto, and Vancouver.
NTXIA will be working with these host cities as well as learning from other cities that previously hosted the event, Sanders says.
AT&T Stadium in Arlington will be one of the main venues for the 2026 tournament. The venue for the big final match hasn’t been announced yet.

title: Wiring Inglewood for the 2026 World Cup: Next-Gen Smart-City Network to Launch this April - PR Newswire
snippet: Wiring Inglewood for the 2026 World Cup: Next-Gen Smart-City Network to Launch this April  PR Newswire
content:
INGLEWOOD, Calif., March 5, 2026 /PRNewswire/ -- In preparation for the 2026 FIFA World Cup and to support the five million annual visitors to its Sports and Entertainment District, a first-of-its-kind digital infrastructure network will debut in Inglewood this April. Developed by Los Angeles-based Out-of-home media and technology company WOW, the new street-level network, named EON, is a patented platform that integrates cinematic brand storytelling with real-time civic utility.
EON introduces a novel, triple-synchronized display technology, allowing multiple digital faces within a single pod to operate collaboratively as one continuous, canvas. Beyond its capabilities as an out-of-home (OOH) media platform, the modular network is engineered to function as a vital smart-city utility. Situated along highly trafficked arterial roads, EON is fully equipped to deliver automated, real-time traffic alerts, event-day detour routing, and emergency public safety messaging to assist motorists and keep the district moving during high-capacity events.
Phase One of the rollout anchors the corridors surrounding major venues - including SoFi Stadium, the Kia Forum, and the Intuit Dome - connecting heavy transit zones along Century Boulevard, Prairie Avenue, Manchester Boulevard, and La Cienega Boulevard. Phase Two, scheduled for later in 2026, will expand the network toward Los Angeles International Airport (LAX) and the 405 Freeway, capturing traffic entering the city from the south and west.
"As cities prepare for global events of unprecedented scale, civic infrastructure must evolve to handle both complex traffic management and the demand for high-fidelity communication," said Scott Krantz, CEO of WOW. "With EON, we are bringing the technological innovation of our skyline spectaculars down to the street level. It is a scalable, intelligent canvas that keeps the city moving while redefining how information is experienced in the public square."
The deployment of EON establishes a new national model for digital civic infrastructure. By integrating the street-level EON network with WOW's existing large-format Spectaculars on the 405 Freeway and surrounding skyline, the company has created the industry's first comprehensive, dual-layer media ecosystem. This allows for synchronized, district-wide messaging that reaches audiences from the highway down to the pedestrian level.
About WOW
WOW is a Los Angeles–based media technology company redefining out-of-home communication through design, data, and innovation. Its patented platform, EON, and its iconic WOW Spectaculars together form a unified media ecosystem that connects brands and cities through intelligent digital infrastructure - fusing architecture, motion, and storytelling into one seamless experience.

title: 2026 World Cup Drives Smart City Evolution in Mexico - Mexico Business News
snippet: 2026 World Cup Drives Smart City Evolution in Mexico  Mexico Business News
content:
2026 World Cup Drives Smart City Evolution in Mexico
The 2026 FIFA World Cup will function as the primary accelerator for hyperconnectivity and data integration in Mexico City, Guadalajara, and Monterrey, driving the transition of these urban centers toward a smart city model that optimizes the citizen experience and critical infrastructure management.
This acceleration addresses a structural necessity to transform digital infrastructure into real-time decision-making ecosystems. Specialists from commercial real estate services company JLL, consultancy Steer, and architecture firm Gensler say that a smart city relies on “its capacity to interpret data and convert it into practical solutions” rather than hardware volume. This perspective shifts the focus from simple coverage to interoperability and strategic information management.
These cities rely on the installed base. For example, Mexico City operates as one of the most connected urban centers globally, with 35,350 free WiFi access points. This network supports speeds ranging from 3Mbps to 5Mbps and connects 1.27 million simultaneous users. While this infrastructure offers an initial competitive advantage, the upcoming international event pressures host cities to evolve from basic connectivity to applied data intelligence.
The central challenge lies in integrating transportation, security, and connectivity into a unified system that mitigates digital access inequalities and ensures operational continuity given the anticipated massive influx of visitors. The strategy for 2026 involves a reengineering of urban management based on three pillars: data intelligence, intermodal mobility, and long-term sustainability.
The integration of information technology and the Internet of Things (IoT) is expected to allow authorities to anticipate mobility patterns and detect incidents in real time. Intelligent captive portals are emerging as key tools in this ecosystem. Beyond facilitating network access, these systems function as bidirectional platforms. While the user receives contextual information regarding safe routes and transportation, the city administration collects aggregated data to optimize urban flows. This process enables a continuous user experience in which connectivity assists the visitor from their arrival at the airport to their entry into the stadium, supported by video surveillance systems and analytical monitoring.
Parallel to digital advances, the physical component of the city faces significant challenges. Experts from JLL, Steer, and Gensler have established that mobility will be the critical test. The requirement is not the construction of new venues but the adaptation of existing infrastructure under a “legacy” scheme. This approach requires the implementation of efficient intermodal transport systems that connect logistic nodes — such as airports, hotels, and stadiums — with urban zones in Guadalajara and Monterrey, reducing the dependence on inefficient transfers.
Sustainability is integrated as an operational requirement rather than an optional feature. Measures include the adoption of Leadership in Energy and Environmental Design (LEED) certifications, circular economy systems for waste management, and energy efficiency through solar power. Furthermore, the plan contemplates social inclusion through adapted infrastructure, such as sensory rooms for people with neurodivergence.
The ultimate objective is for the investment and technical planning of the 2026 World Cup to result in permanent infrastructure. This legacy aims to consolidate more resilient, connected, and competitive cities that attract foreign investment long after the tournament concludes.
Global Benchmarks: Legacy in Previous Host Cities
The 2022 World Cup in Qatar set a global benchmark for centralized urban management through the deployment of the Aspire Command and Control Centre. This facility integrated data from over 20,000 security cameras and thousands of IoT sensors across eight stadiums and major transport hubs into a single interface. The system used AI to analyze crowd flow, building operations, and traffic density in real time. This infrastructure transitioned post-tournament into the operational backbone of Lusail City, a master-planned municipality designed to function as a fully cognitive smart city.
In preparation for the 2018 tournament in Russia, Moscow accelerated the implementation of its "Safe City" initiative, a massive upgrade to its digital surveillance and connectivity infrastructure. The city integrated facial recognition software with a network of more than 160,000 CCTV cameras to monitor security and optimize traffic management during the event. Furthermore, the authorities upgraded the telecommunications network within the Moscow Metro to support high-density public WiFi and seamless contactless payments. These investments established a permanent framework for data-driven municipal governance and digital adoption that remains active today.
Brazil 2014 drove the establishment of Integrated Command and Control Centers (CICCs) across 12 host cities to address complex security challenges. These hubs aggregated real-time data from police, fire, traffic, and medical emergency departments into a single interoperable platform, breaking down historical information silos between agencies. While the sporting event served as the catalyst, the physical and digital infrastructure of the CICCs became a permanent asset for Brazilian public safety, permanently altering how local governments coordinate emergency responses and analyze security data.

When football calls, Ghana answers.
In June 2026, the world’s greatest football festival will unfold across North America, with matches in the United States, Canada and Mexico. In essence, the trip is designed not only as a sporting pilgrimage but also as a gateway for Ghanaian brands to meet the world.
Major tournaments demand more than enthusiasm; they require planning, access and local knowledge.
Litina Travels offers:
- Secured accommodation near match venues
- Guaranteed match ticket allocation within packages
- Structured visa support
- Travel insurance coverage
- Coordinated airport and stadium transfers

For Ghanaian supporters, the difference lies in certainty. In a World Cup year, flights fill up. Hotels disappear. Prices escalate. Early, organised group travel reduces those risks.
The 2026 tournament will be the first ever hosted across three nations. It will be expansive, complex and historic.
For fans who want to be present when Ghana steps onto that stage — when the anthem plays in Toronto, when the stakes rise in Boston, when pride echoes in Philadelphia — preparation is everything.
Litina Travels is betting that when the world gathers for greatness, Ghana will travel with purpose.
And in 2026, that journey may well begin at Alisa Hotel, North Ridge, with a passport, a dream and a ticket to history.
Just walk in.

title: FIFA Unveils Trionda The Smart Ball for the 2026 World Cup - prameyanews.com
snippet: FIFA Unveils Trionda The Smart Ball for the 2026 World Cup  prameyanews.com
content:
FIFA Unveils Trionda The High-Tech Heart of the 2026 World Cup
FIFA has officially unveiled 'Trionda,' the technologically advanced match ball for the 2026 World Cup, which is as much a symbol of unity as it is a tool for precision officiating. The ball's design pays tribute to the tournament's three host nations—Canada, Mexico, and the United States—while its internal hardware is set to provide match officials with unprecedented levels of data to ensure a fairer game.
A Design Celebrating Three Nations
The name 'Trionda' is derived from the Spanish for "three waves," a direct reference to the historic tri-nation hosting of the event. The ball's aesthetic reinforces this theme, with a vibrant red, green, and blue color scheme that honors the national colors of the hosts.
The design is rich with symbolism. The fluid, four-panel construction is adorned with specific iconography representing each country a maple leaf for Canada, an eagle for Mexico, and a star for the United States. Gold embellishments are woven into the design as a nod to the iconic World Cup trophy, completing a look that is both modern and meaningful.
The 'Heartbeat' Within the Ball
The most groundbreaking feature of the Trionda, however, lies hidden beneath its surface. Continuing the evolution of connected ball technology, the ball contains a sophisticated 500Hz inertial measurement unit (IMU) motion sensor chip, complete with an accelerometer and a gyroscope. An Adidas technician aptly described the technology as giving a "heartbeat to the ball," as it sends a signal 500 times every second to anchor points around the stadium.
This technology is engineered for a single purpose to make the game fairer. The chip is capable of detecting every minute touch, deflection, or spin. This real-time data is fed directly to the Video Assistant Referee (VAR) system, providing an objective and instantaneous source of information for crucial decisions.
Enhancing Officiating with Precision Data
The practical applications of this technology are game-changing, particularly for two of football's most contentious calls offsides and handballs. For offside decisions, the sensor provides the precise "kick point" of a pass with an accuracy that is impossible to achieve by reviewing television footage alone. This data, combined with player tracking technology, allows for faster and more accurate rulings.
Similarly, in crowded penalty areas where a referee's view may be obstructed, the chip can confirm if the ball made contact with a player's hand or arm, removing ambiguity from critical penalty decisions. To ensure this internal hardware doesn't affect the ball's flight or balance, the chip is ingeniously mounted in one of the four panels, with counter-balances placed in the other three, a significant evolution from previous designs.

title: Artificial intelligence changes offside decisions at the 2026 World Cup - thecanary.co
snippet: Artificial intelligence changes offside decisions at the 2026 World Cup  thecanary.co
content:
FIFA and Lenovo announced a package of AI-powered technological innovations in preparation for the FIFA World Cup 2026 during the Lenovo Tech World 2026 event held in Las Vegas on the sidelines of CES.
World Cup 2026 to use AI for refereeing decisions
The innovations included the launch of Football AI Pro, a generative AI-powered knowledge assistant designed to support all 48 teams participating in the tournament with advanced and equal analytical capabilities before and after matches, by analysing hundreds of millions of FIFA-owned football data points and providing multiple outputs including text, videos, graphics and 3D footage in several languages.
The two parties also unveiled AI-powered 3D player avatars, which are used to develop semi-automated offside technology by digitally scanning players in approximately one second, enabling high-precision tracking of their movements and improving the speed and accuracy of offside decisions. These models will be integrated into television broadcasts to explain VAR decisions more clearly to fans.
FIFA and Lenovo also announced an upgraded version of the referee camera broadcast system, based on AI-powered stabilisation technologies, enabling live, higher-quality footage and reduced motion blur, with the aim of enhancing transparency and viewer engagement.
Both parties confirmed that these innovations are part of FIFA’s digital transformation strategy for 2023–2027. and with the official partnership with Lenovo as the technology partner for the FIFA World Cup 2026 and the FIFA Women’s World Cup 2027, where Lenovo will provide the infrastructure, devices and AI-powered smart solutions for both events.
These technologies aim to improve refereeing, develop performance analysis, and deliver a more interactive and immersive experience for fans in the largest edition of the World Cup in history, with 48 teams participating and 104 matches being played.

title: England leaning on Artificial Intelligence for vital edge at 2026 FIFA World Cup - The Indian Express
snippet: England leaning on Artificial Intelligence for vital edge at 2026 FIFA World Cup  The Indian Express
content:
England haven’t been football World Champions since 1966, and are looking at every avenue that can give them an edge to end their long drought in North America next year.
Artificial Intelligence (AI) is being used in almost every facet of human endeavour and the Three Lions are banking on it to turn from contenders to winners.
From taking penalties to tactical decisions to looking after players’ wellbeing, the decision-makers are determined to leave nothing to chance, BBC Sport reports.
Penalty shootouts have given England a lot of grief over the years, as they have often bowed out of World Cups or European Championships. Even at Qatar 2022, in the quarterfinal against France, Harry Kane missed a late penalty in the near-miss.
“AI can show certain tendencies for where opposition players put their penalties that we probably weren’t thinking of,” explains Rhys Long, the Football Association’s head of performance insights and analysis.
“When we get to a World Cup, we have 47 teams’ worth of information to profile – where has every player in every squad put every penalty since they were 16?”
The new technology has made it possible to collate information much quicker than before.
“It used to take us five days to collect one team’s worth of penalty-taking information. Using AI, that can now be brought down to about five hours. Then that becomes a five-minute conversation with our goalkeeper, for five seconds of them hopefully saving a penalty.”
It also helps penalty-takers to do what is required of them.
“There was a diagram up on the board of where you’re more likely to score, then they would give you individualised information on where they think is best for you to go,” Conor Coady, a member of the 2020 Euros and 2022 World Cup, said.
The diagrams are based on what every opposition goalkeeper is likely to do, such as the direction in which they are going to dive, and how each England player prefers to strike the ball.
Tactics and in-game patterns are areas where AI is being used, and England is one of the countries to have invested heavily in it, and more importantly, the players seem to have bought into it.
“England have a big resource and have heavily invested in this. They have data engineers, data analysts and performance specialists behind the scenes across all their teams from juniors up to senior,” Allistair McRobert, professor of performance analysis at Liverpool John Moores University, was quoted as saying.
Players’ wellbeing, fitness and prevention of injuries are other areas where AI is helping.
“You wake up every morning and as you’re going down to breakfast, there is a wellness area where you fill out a form on an iPad,” Coady said.
“’How did you sleep? How did you feel this morning? Are you fatigued?’ And then you leave comments on it – maybe ‘my hamstrings are sore from training yesterday’. And then the staff cater for you during the day, in terms of what you need in training, your food, how they set up a session.”

content:
Senegal, DRC, Sudan…: Artificial Intelligence reveals the favorite to qualify for the 2026 World Cup
The battle is raging in Group B of the African qualifiers for the 2026 World Cup. After the 7th matchday, the DRC leads the group with 16 points, closely followed by Senegal (15 pts) and Sudan (12 pts). Three nations vying for, potentially, one direct spot for the World Cup.
In this context, a predictive analysis generated by Artificial Intelligence has shed interesting light on the qualification chances of each team. Based on current form, remaining schedule, offensive/defensive statistics, and previous encounters, this sports-specialized AI makes its predictions.
According to the projections of Artificial Intelligence, Senegal has 55% chance of directly qualifying for the 2026 World Cup, compared to 40% for the DRC. Sudan, despite its 12 points, only has a 5% probability of finishing on top of the group. As for a potential playoff scenario, the estimations give 35% chance to Senegal, 45% to DR Congo, and 20% to Sudan. Despite its current second place, Senegal benefits from a better goal difference (+9), a team experienced in big games, and two home matches in the last three. DRC, the solid leader, faces a slightly tougher schedule, including a direct clash against Senegal.
Sudan, lurking behind, still holds mathematical hopes, but will need to have an almost perfect run to hope to surpass one of the two favorites.
A decisive Senegal – DRC showdown
According to the AI, the upcoming clash Senegal – DRC could be the turning point of the group. The winner of this duel will have one foot in the 2026 World Cup, while the third-placed team (Sudan) will play the role of spoiler. Nothing is set in stone yet, but the Lions of Teranga seem to have all the cards in hand. The truth will be revealed on the field… maybe by just one goal.

title: Mexico Builds Digital Infrastructure for 2026 World Cup - The Tech Buzz
snippet: Mexico Builds Digital Infrastructure for 2026 World Cup  The Tech Buzz
content:
Mexico is rolling out digital tools to handle the expected 5.5 million visitors for the 2026 FIFA World Cup. The government just unveiled plans for a bilingual tourism app called "Conoce México" and an official ticket resale platform developed in partnership with FIFA. These platforms represent Mexico's bid to modernize its tourism infrastructure while preventing ticket scalping through legitimate digital channels.
Mexico just dropped its digital playbook for hosting the 2026 FIFA World Cup, and it's banking on apps to manage what could be the largest tourism surge in the country's history. President Claudia Sheinbaum announced the "Mexico 2026 Social World Cup" project last week, centering on two key digital platforms that aim to transform how millions of visitors experience the tournament.
The centerpiece is "Conoce México" (Get to Know Mexico), a bilingual tourism app developed jointly by Mexico's Agency for Digital Transformation and Telecommunications and the Ministry of Tourism. The platform promises an "intuitive and accessible interface" that goes beyond basic match information to include cultural events, gastronomic recommendations, and ecotourism options across host cities.
But the real innovation lies in the app's interactive tourist atlas, which maps over 260 fan routes, points of interest, and thematic roads. This isn't just a digital brochure - it's positioning itself as the central nervous system for tourist navigation during the tournament. The app will deliver real-time match results, stadium details, transportation information, and connectivity guides, all designed for the 5.5 million domestic and international visitors Mexico expects to receive.
The timing is critical. This World Cup marks the first time in over 90 years that three countries will co-host, with 48 teams playing 104 matches across Mexico, the United States, and Canada. That's 16 more teams than previous tournaments and represents a massive logistical challenge that traditional tourism infrastructure simply can't handle.
Parallel to the tourism push, FIFA and Mexico's Federal Consumer Protection Agency (Profeco) are developing what they're calling a "ticket relocation system" - essentially an official resale marketplace. Iván Escalante, who heads Profeco, announced the platform will operate in Spanish and display prices in Mexican pesos, complying with national legislation while providing a legitimate alternative to informal ticket scalping.

title: Fort Worth officials brace for 2026 World Cup’s strain on infrastructure, economic boom - Fort Worth Report
snippet: Fort Worth officials brace for 2026 World Cup’s strain on infrastructure, economic boom  Fort Worth Report
content:
Hosting the World Cup is an operation arguably larger than any Super Bowl, political convention or Olympics, proposed Mike Crum, Fort Worth director of public events.
The soccer spectacle taking place in Arlington next year is expected to bring $1.5 billion and over 100,000 daily visitors to the region, so Crum told City Council members Tuesday that the area is bracing for the economic opportunities as well as strains on public transportation and other resources.
“The World Cup is the most watched event in the world, so the world’s eyes are going to be on the metroplex during June and July of this coming summer,” Crum said.
As Arlington prepares to host nine FIFA World Cup games next year — taking place June 11 through July 19 — officials detailed how Fort Worth is planning to navigate related challenges.
The city is launching work groups to plan for the World Cup with external stakeholders, said Sonny Saxton, director of emergency communications and management, at Tuesday’s work session. The groups will focus on a range of issues including communications, engagement, hospitality, beautification, safety and infrastructure such as roads and city services.
A potential major factor for how the World Cup affects the city will be if a country selects Texas Christian University as a base camp, said Mitch Whitten, chief operating officer of Visit Fort Worth. That would bring its fans, corporate partners and media attention to Fort Worth, he said. Those selections are expected to be revealed in December.
In the meantime, Whitten said Trinity Metro is planning to accommodate high traffic with new lines and services. He told council members he couldn’t speak to broader collaboration between transit entities.
Visit Fort Worth launched nationwide efforts to pitch the city’s attractions to media outlets, he said. On Tuesday, the group launched its soccer website where prospective visitors can plan itineraries and coordinate stays and transit.
During a July meeting, emergency medicine expert Brian Miller described the World Cup to Fort Worth’s Medical Control Advisory Board as the equivalent of multiple Super Bowls happening over a month.
Many U.S. residents don’t realize how big the World Cup is, he added, warning that the soccer fandom can get rowdier than the NFL.
“We’re going to be street sweeping all the time and cleaning up and just trying to get our heads around that,” Miller said.
Council member Deborah Peoples, who represents far east Fort Worth, stretching to Arlington and the DFW Airport, said she hopes officials advertise areas beyond downtown and the Stockyards.
“I want us to make an effort to make sure that we’re showcasing all areas of the city,” Peoples told the Report, naming east Fort Worth’s Handley Wedding District and District 11’s Gateway Park as two attractions that should be highlighted.
Saxton said city staffers are working to balance visitors with residents and to mitigate how the World Cup affects community members.
“(We) want to be sure the essential services they depend upon remain uninterrupted throughout the event,” Saxton said. “I’m thinking about things like transportation, public safety and day-to-day operations that need to continue to run smoothly.”
Fort Worth’s environmental services team worked with external North Texas FIFA committees to clean up Lake Arlington in June. Saxton said the city can expect to see more of such initiatives.
The city’s transit, aviation and medical professionals, as well as experts on homelessness, are coordinating planning efforts, he added. More details on the results of their planning will be presented to City Council at a later date.
Medical officials at a July board meeting specifically pointed out that July 3, 2026, will be a challenging day. It’s a Friday, the eve of the United States’ 250th birthday, a World Cup match and a convention in Fort Worth’s downtown.
“A lot of work is going on with no financial support coming from the federal government,” Fire Chief Jim Davis said at the advisory board meeting.
The city’s fire department and emergency response teams have spent the past two years streamlining and consolidating operations to be more efficient.
The fire department’s new in-house ambulance services launched in July, and the city established a new Emergency Management & Communications Department, which coordinates 911 call-taking and emergency preparation.
The first World Cup tickets went on sale Sept. 10.
Disclosure: Mitch Whitten is on the Fort Worth Report’s board of directors. The Report’s news decisions are made independently of our board members and financial supporters. Read more about our editorial independence policy here.

title: France, Germany, Ireland And Canada Advise U.S. Tourists to Plan Early for FIFA World Cup 2026 as Record-Breaking Global Travel, Increased Security, and Strained Infrastructure Take Center Stage - Travel And Tour World
snippet: France, Germany, Ireland And Canada Advise U.S. Tourists to Plan Early for FIFA World Cup 2026 as Record-Breaking Global Travel, Increased Security, and Strained Infrastructure Take Center Stage  Travel And Tour World
content:
Published on February 13, 2026
As the FIFA World Cup 2026 approaches, several European countries, including France, Germany, Ireland, and Canada, have issued updated travel advisories for the U.S. due to the anticipated impact of the tournament. These advisories highlight the expected challenges travelers may face, such as heightened security, increased airport congestion, and potential delays. With the tournament being hosted across multiple cities in North America and involving 48 teams, the scale of the event is expected to place significant strain on transportation systems, accommodations, and security protocols. Travelers are urged to plan well in advance, ensure all travel documents are in order, and stay informed of the latest updates to avoid disruptions during this high-demand period.
Several European governments have updated their travel advisories for the United States as the 2026 FIFA World Cup draws nearer, drawing increased attention from tour operators, airlines, and travelers planning international trips around the highly anticipated tournament. The World Cup, set to be hosted across multiple cities in North America with 48 teams participating, is expected to significantly impact both domestic and international travel dynamics.
Countries including France, Germany, Ireland, and Canada have all revised their travel guidelines for the U.S., highlighting potential challenges related to congestion, heightened security, and logistical concerns due to the scale of the event. These advisories don’t discourage travel but urge travelers to stay vigilant, plan their trips well in advance, and adhere strictly to all entry and security protocols.
Travel advisors and airlines are already anticipating the effects of these updates on travel behavior. Although the advisories don’t outright discourage travel, they serve as a reminder to prepare for the busy, high-demand period. Travelers to the U.S. are warned that the World Cup’s extensive fan base will result in crowded venues, airports, and borders, as well as longer wait times for security screenings. Moreover, many host cities are likely to experience a surge in accommodation prices during key match dates, making it essential for international visitors to book well ahead of time.
Advertisement
Advertisement
The revised advisories stress that travelers should ensure their travel documents are in order well before departure. The French Ministry of Foreign Affairs, for example, has emphasized the importance of being aware of potential security risks in crowded areas, while also urging travelers to confirm the validity of visas and other necessary authorizations. Germany’s updated guidance echoes these concerns, advising travelers to stay informed through local news channels and official sources during major events such as the World Cup.
These travel warnings are not just limited to international visitors heading to the U.S. U.S.-based travelers are already feeling the ripple effects of the advisories. Some Americans, particularly those planning multi-country European vacations in 2026, are reconsidering their travel plans to avoid the possibility of congestion and soaring prices. Many are either advancing their trips to 2025 or postponing until after the tournament to steer clear of the logistical challenges likely to arise during the World Cup’s peak periods.
Advertisement
Advertisement
Airline analysts have pointed out that even though these advisories do not directly alter policies, the tone of government language can influence how travelers perceive their trips. For risk-averse individuals and families, the updates often result in a shift in booking habits. The mere mention of heightened security and travel disruptions can lead to delays in bookings or a preference for less busy travel periods, especially when it comes to transatlantic flights.
The increased pressure on airlines during the World Cup is expected to have a significant impact on flight availability and prices. With millions of visitors expected to travel between the U.S., Canada, and Mexico, European carriers are likely to adjust their summer schedules to prioritize high-demand routes. This could lead to fewer available seats on popular U.S.–Europe flights and result in price hikes, particularly during match windows when demand is at its peak.
At the same time, experts within the travel industry stress that such advisories are a standard part of the process when preparing for large-scale international events. Governments routinely issue these warnings ahead of time to ensure that travelers are well-informed about potential risks, rather than to create panic. The message from European authorities remains consistent: travelers should prepare early, stay updated with official guidance, and be prepared for a spike in demand as the tournament approaches.
For tourism boards and private operators across Europe and North America, these travel advisories are a sign that the 2026 World Cup is already shaping the global tourism landscape. Both sides of the Atlantic are closely monitoring booking data and travel patterns, adjusting strategies accordingly. This intersection of heightened security measures, infrastructure strain, and record-breaking demand is already influencing travel behaviors for 2026 — months ahead of the event itself.
As the World Cup approaches, travelers are advised to stay ahead of the curve. While the advisories serve as a reminder of the logistical and security challenges, they also underscore the importance of early planning. The global attention on the World Cup is undoubtedly transforming how people view travel to the U.S. and other participating countries. The ripple effect of these updates will continue to shape travel decisions well into 2026, setting the stage for a busy, bustling tournament year.
